title: US to close flagship Gaza monitoring mission as Trump administration plan stalls
summary: Reuters reports the US military-run center near Gaza designed to monitor the Israel-Hamas ceasefire and boost aid flows will shut down. Critics noted the mission failed to achieve its stated objectives.
contents:
# US Closes Flagship Gaza Monitoring Mission

## Exclusive Report
The Trump administration plans to close the US military-run center near Gaza, according to sources familiar with the matter. The facility, established to monitor the Israel-Hamas ceasefire and boost humanitarian aid flows to besieged Palestinians, reportedly failed in its core mission.

## Background
- The center served as Washington's flagship civilian-military coordination facility for the Gaza crisis
- Critics consistently pointed to the facility's inability to prevent ongoing Israeli military operations despite the ceasefire framework
- The closure signals a broader US disengagement from Gaza humanitarian oversight

## Broader Context
The decision comes amid multiple competing demands on US military resources, including the ongoing war with Iran and the Strait of Hormuz blockade. The Trump administration faces congressional pressure over the $25 billion cost of the Iran conflict while managing the fragile Gaza ceasefire.

## Implications
The closure removes a key US monitoring presence at a time when approximately 2 million Gaza residents continue to face severe humanitarian conditions. Aid organizations expressed concern about reduced accountability for ceasefire violations in the absence of US monitoring infrastructure.
